request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "The Abandoned Manor" (id: the-abandoned-manor), level 1.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- 77 | printed key "77" | Gateway Flanked by Towers | pages 41
- 78 | printed key "78" | West Gate Tower | pages 41
- 79 | printed key "79" | East Gate Tower | pages 41
- 80 | printed key "80" | Stables | pages 41
- 81 | printed key "81" | Cottages | pages 42
- 82 | printed key "82" | North Wall | pages 42
- 83 | printed key "83" | Moat | pages 42
- 84 | printed key "84" | Main Doors | pages 42
- 85 | printed key "85" | Main Hall | pages 42, 43
- 86 | printed key "86" | Renovated Stables | pages 43
- 87 | printed key "87" | Wagon Storage | pages 43
- 88 | printed key "88" | Prep Room | pages 43
- 89 | printed key "89" | Kitchen | pages 43
- 90 | printed key "90" | Store Room | pages 44
- 91 | printed key "91" | Laundry Room | pages 44
- 92 | printed key "92" | Wash Room | pages 44
- 93 | printed key "93" | Back Door | pages 44
- 94 | printed key "94" | Servant Quarters | pages 44
- 95 | printed key "95" | Servant Quarters | pages 44
- 96 | printed key "96" | Servant Quarters | pages 44
- 97 | printed key "97" | Servant Quarters | pages 44
This level's map is on page 46.

[page 41]
The Chaotic Caves The Abandoned Manor
MAP K – MANOR FORT GROUND
77. GATEWAY FLANKED BY TOWERS: A
corroded brass plaque over the gates reads
"Roslof Manor." The old wooden gates have
been torn aside, battered down long ago in a
forgotten fight which led to the manor being
abandoned. Recently (within the last few
days) someone has taken out the stronger
timbers and much of the metal hardware. The
rest is laid out as a pattern, as if plans are
being made to replace them.
The area has been cleaned up somewhat for
easy access. Hoof prints and wagon tracks
point in and out on the soft earth, amid badlybroken paving stones.
78. WEST GATE TOWER: This tower is three
stories high (counting the roof as the fourth),
but the top floors have collapsed inward,
leaving only the bottom one. It is a simple
hollow tube with a steep stairway that would
provide access to each floor if they were still
intact. Arrow slits are every 10 feet, positioned
where they can face out. The entrance door
has a lock but is not kept locked; a heavy bar
is kept beside it to fit into brackets on the
frame.
This is a guard post for human fighters wearing
scarlet cloaks, red-enameled helmets, and
chain mail. Each has 1d6 gp. They are busy
dealing with a giant black widow spider that
just crawled into the rubble of the tower
overhead. It might come scuttling down the
wall to attack the PCs!
Human Fighter 2: AC 15, AB +2, #At 1 
longsword, Dam 1d8 (1d8+1 for one for sword 
+1), Mv 20', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
6 Human Fighter 1: AC 15, AB +1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 20', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
Giant Black Widow Spider: AC 14, HD 3*, 
#At 1 bite, Dam 2d6 + poison, Mv 20' 40' web, 
Sv F3, ML 8, XP 175
HP 12 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
79. EAST GATE TOWER: As #78 except It has
been badly damaged and is not safe to use; no
humans are found here. The door has a lock,
but it has been broken off its hinges and leans
askew in the frame, wedged in place by heavy
wooden pegs.
The lower floor is occupied by giant
centipedes, crawling amid rubble. The rubble
of the next floor is overrun with giant rats
scurrying amid broken furniture and various
skeletal remains (harmless). 
9 Giant Centipedes: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), 
#At 1 bite, Dam poison, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7, 
XP 10 ea.
HP 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
9 Giant Rats: AC 13, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 bite, 
Dam 1d4+disease, Mv 40' swim 20', Sv F1, ML 8,
XP 10 ea.
HP 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
80. STABLES: These stables are in ruins from
neglect. There was once a smithy, small
cottages, hay storage, wagon storage, rooms
for grooms, and so on. The structures have
collapsed, leaving a maze of darkness and ruin.
Soot and burned timbers, the signs of ancient
fires, are everywhere. New weeds are growing
through the rubbish.
A giant weasel is sparring with three giant
shrews that have killed a pack of giant rats and
are going to feast on them. If they smell fresh
meat, they will attack it instead.
Giant Weasel: AC 17, HD 5, #At 1 bite + hold, 
Dam 2d4 bite + 2d4/round hold (rending), 
Mv 50', Sv F5, ML 8, XP 360
HP 22 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐
3 Giant Shrews: AC 16, HD 1, #At 2 bites, 
Dam 1d6 bite, Mv 60', Sv F2, ML 10, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
39

[page 42]
The Abandoned Manor The Chaotic Caves
81. COTTAGES: These were quarters for
gardeners and other staff, but they are now in
ruins, much like #80. Berries, fruit trees, and
vegetables are growing wild. During the
growing season, the player characters can
stock up on 1d6 weeks of rations quite easily,
most of which will spoil in a week or two
unless dried or otherwise preserved.
82. NORTH WALL: This collection of rotten
wooden buildings and weeds mark the area
once used for dog kennels. 
The wall in this area is patrolled by the
minotaur ally of the humans. If it notices the
player characters, it will bellow to try to
attract guards and hurl a few rocks (for 1d6
rounds or as reasonable) then charge down the
closest set of steps (randomly roll if need be).
If the GM needs rules for rock throwing:
Rock Damage Range
light 1d6 30/60/90
medium 1d8 20/40/60
boulder 1d10 10/20/30
It wears a necklace of 100 gp and 50 pp (these
were minted with a hole in the center and
weigh as much as normal coins). A rude leanto of hides stands on the northeast corner,
under which is the half-eaten, raw carcass of a
deer. Various animal parts are strewn about.
Minotaur: AC 14, HD 6, #At 1 gore, 1 bite, or 1 
battle axe Dam 1d6 gore, 1d6 bite, 1d8+1 battle 
axe, Mv 30', Sv F6, ML 11, XP 500
HP 27 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
83. MOAT: The main house relies on a 10' deep
moat for security (it is currently full), with a
simple wooden bridge crossing it at the front
entrance. The bridge is gone, burned down
long ago, leaving only the planks and timbers
of the footing on each side. The moat is fed by
a slow-moving stream that comes under the
north wall and exits to the east. Each
entrance is serviced by an arched undercut
with two sets of heavy iron bars (one on each
side of the opening) to help keep out intruders.
84. MAIN DOORS: The great iron double doors
of the main house are firmly shut and barred
from the inside. Short of using a massive ram,
they cannot be opened. There are quite a
number of high, wide windows along the walls,
with heavy iron bars keeping out anything
bigger than a squirrel. Ripping out the window
bars (or using some other noisy method to get
in) would likely attract a lot of attention from
occupants inside.
If the party looks around, they find that some
of the blocks on the west side of the door can
be easily pulled out of the walls. Invaders had
knocked a hole there and the blocks were
stacked sloppily when the house was restored
by its current denizens. The characters can
easily open holes in the wall and step inside
into room #85.
To one side lie three wooden ramps, each
about 24 feet long and made from timbers and
iron hardware collected from elsewhere. They
allow wagons to cross the moat.
85. MAIN HALL: The lower floor has been used
hard. Many of the hardwood timbers are torn
out or scuffed with abuse and dirt is
everywhere. Upstairs areas are less damaged
and the rooms are cleaner.
A heavy iron chandelier hangs overhead for
lanterns or torches. There are also small wall
mounts here and there throughout the manor.
Skylights illuminate the main hall well during
the day; most of the rooms in the manor have
windows on the outside facing walls for plenty
of light during the day.
Two sets of ornate stairs ("a" and "b") lead up
to second floor balcony (see entry #98 on the
second floor map), going up some 20 feet.
Under the balcony are swinging double doors
that lead into the back. The main hall ceiling is
some 30' high. The ceilings in other rooms are
20' high.
Recent bootprints in the dust on the floor lead
everywhere. The walls of some of the
adjoining rooms were once of thick hardwood
but have been torn down in places. Horses can
be heard snorting: read entry #86 and #87 for
more.
As the adventurers enter, three people wearing
red surcoats over chain mail and carrying
swords are met coming down the stairs. They
are discussing whether they can get richer
40

[page 43]
The Chaotic Caves The Abandoned Manor
raiding towns or by waylaying travelers
(whether or not they note the adventurers
depends on what the PCs are doing and what
the GM wishes). One is a woman, and she is
armed with a longsword +1. They will try to
lure the party into an ambush if the player's
aren't wary: one will excuse himself and go to
room #88 to get the troops there, while the
others lead the party upstairs to #106 before
attacking.
3 Fighters: AC 15, F2, #At 1 weapon, Dam 1d8 
(1d8+1 for one for longword +1), Mv 20', Sv F2, 
ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
A smith and two assistants working in the
stables will come join them at a shout or any
noise of a fight.
Smith, Human Fighter 2: AC 13, AB +2, 
#At 1 hammer, Dam 1d6+1 (bonus for strength), 
Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Apprentice Smiths, Human Fighter 1: AC 13, 
AB +1, #At 1 hammer, Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, 
ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
86. RENOVATED STABLES: These rooms were
once a library, music room, and gymnasium,
but the space has been gutted and affixed with
simple wooden stalls to serve as a smithy and
stables. There are four riding horses, three
mules, and three oxen here. Hay, feed bags,
sacks of feed grain, and other items are
nearby. In one corner are a small traveling
anvil, a pile of bricks, bags of coal, several
hammers, and other tools, along with a few
horse shoes. Two horses were lost to
wandering monsters before the rest of the
animals were brought inside for safe keeping.
87. WAGON STORAGE: The south end of this
area was once a posh sitting room. The walls
were knocked out to make room for wagons
and other equipment. There are two wagons
and two carts here, plus boxes, tarps, and
other items.
The northern end was once a long dining hall.
Old, smashed tables and chairs are piled up in
neat stacks arranged like firewood in short
lengths. Some of the strong, thicker pieces are
arranged like building timbers ready for use.
There is plenty of room between the piles to
get to and from the kitchen and prep room in
back.
88. PREP ROOM: Once a prep room for meals
ready to bring into the dining room (and to
buffer the racket and business of the kitchen),
this room now holds tables and chairs, usually
with dirty wooden plates and pewter or
wooden mugs and other items on them. A keg
of cheap beer is propped on a table in one
corner.
A group of men are sitting down to a meal of
bread, meat, gravy, and cheap beer. The sound
of a fight will bring two more men running
from the kitchen (see room #89). The leader
has chain mail and a sword. The others wear
red-stained leather armor and have various
weapons (short swords, hand axes, and war
hammers). Each has 2d6 gp on him.
Sergeant, Human Fighter 2: AC 15, AB +2, 
#At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F2, ML 8, 
XP 75
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
6 Human Fighter 1: AC 13, AB +1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 7 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
89. KITCHEN: Appointed to cook large meals,
with several ovens and stoves along the outer
walls. There are plenty of tables, sinks, etc.
Dirty wooden dishes are common here. 
There are two men here cooking a stew on one
of the stoves. They will join the six in room
#88 if a fight breaks out. They are armed with
hand axes and dressed in red-stained leather
armor. Each has 2d6 gp on him.
2 Human Fighter 1: AC 13, AB +1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
41

[page 44]
The Abandoned Manor The Chaotic Caves
90. STORE ROOM: For dry foods and such.
Some bags of flour and other grains and
vegetables are stacked here. A narrow set of
stairs leads down 10' to a cold room where the
fresh carcasses of venison, boar, rabbits, and
other game animals hang on iron hooks.
91. LAUNDRY ROOM: Buckets and tubs once
resided here. Now there are only long ropes to
hang laundry up to dry. Wooden shelves for
linens and rugs gawk emptily at the observer.
In one corner are two sealed buckets: one
contains red paint and the other red varnish.
92. WASH ROOM: For humans, clothes, dishes,
etc. It contains bath tubs, cloth screens on
wooden frames, and a table with bars of soap.
93. BACK DOOR. The rear exit. The door is a
heavy security door with two iron bars that can
be thrown across from the inside.
94 to 97. SERVANT QUARTERS: These
chambers once held servants and their guests
and are now occupied in part by foot troops,
most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The
inner partitions of each set have been torn
down to enlarge the rooms.
In room #94, two men are resting after a fight
with giant rats. They can still fight and will
accost intruders to the best of their ability
(they are not wearing their leather armor but
can put it on, given time).
2 Human Fighter 1: AC 13 or 11, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30' or 40', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
As the adventurers enter the hall around #97, a
man will come out of the door and then
(unless stopped) duck back inside and call for
help. Four men occupy this room, sleeping
after watch. Their spears and hand axes are
nearby. They are not currently wearing their
red-stained leather armor, but will put it on if
given time. Each has 2d6 gp on him. 
4 Human Fighter 1: AC 13, AB +1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
MAP L – MANOR UPPER FLOOR
The upper floor is occupied by the leaders of
this band of fighters. Each room has several
gas lights, tapped from a natural deposit of gas
in the rock far below, as well as windows and
torch holders. The ceilings of the rooms on
this floor are about 10' high (the stairs rise 20').
98. STAIRWAY: The stairs go down into the
ground floor to #85. A railing along the side
provides safety.
99. GUEST ROOM: This is the residence of the
female fighter and one of the male fighters
who are met coming down the stairs in entry
#85. Furnishings include makeshift bed, table,
chairs, and such. There is a single locked
casket here (with a crude lock only intended to
indicate tampering, not really keep anyone out)
with 345 gp and five gems worth 50 gp each.
100. GUEST ROOM: One of the three fighters
encountered on the stairs (entry #85 on the
ground floor) lives here. It is furnished by a
makeshift bed, table, chairs, and such. Under
the bed is a sack with ten gems worth 50 gp
each, plus coins: 198 gp and 31 sp. It is being
guarded by the owner's pet snake which might
immediately grab and pull someone under if
they investigate unwisely.
Python: AC 14, HD 5*, #At 1 bite, 1 constrict, 
Dam 1d4 bite, 2d4 + 2d4/round constrict, 
Mv 30', Sv F5, ML 8, XP 405
HP 20 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
101. GUEST ROOM: Now being used by one of
the clerics as a private room (see #106). It has
makeshift furnishings: bed, table, chairs, etc.
On a chest of drawers are three silver holy
symbols (worth 25 gp each) arranged in a
worshipful way around a golden icon of some
evil demonic being (valued at 100 gp) known
for austerity and obedience.
102. GUEST ROOM: Now claimed by one of the
clerics as a private room (see #106). It
contains shabby, slapped-together furniture:
bed, table, chairs, and such. Hung on one wall
is a painting of horrors and stark terror that in
general has no value. A church of good nature
might buy it for 100 gp or so in order to burn it
or use it as an educational tool to illustrate the
true face of evil. Hung under it are a holy
42

[page 46]
The Abandoned Manor The Chaotic Caves
44

areas.description: A corroded brass plaque over the gates reads "Roslof Manor." The old wooden gates were battered down long ago and recently someone removed the stronger timbers and much of the metal hardware, laying the rest out in a pattern as if preparing to replace them. The area has been cleaned for easy access; hoofprints and wagon tracks mark the soft earth among badly broken paving stones.
features: Ruined wooden gates with salvaged timbers and metal hardware laid out for replacement

areas.description: A gate tower originally three stories high plus roof; the top floors have collapsed inward, leaving only the bottom floor. It is a hollow tube with a steep stairway that would have reached each floor, and arrow slits every 10 feet facing outward. The entrance door has a lock but is not kept locked; a heavy bar lies beside it to fit brackets on the frame. This tower serves as a guard post for human fighters in scarlet cloaks, red-enameled helmets, and chain mail. They are currently dealing with a giant black widow spider in the rubble overhead, which may scuttle down the wall to attack.
features: Door with lock and nearby heavy bar

areas.description: Like area 78, but this tower has been badly damaged and is unsafe to use. No humans are stationed here. The door's lock is broken off its hinges and the door leans askew in the frame, wedged in place by heavy wooden pegs. The lower floor is full of rubble occupied by giant centipedes. The rubble of the next floor is overrun with giant rats amid broken furniture and harmless skeletal remains.

encounters.count_note: in the rubble of the next floor

features: Broken door wedged in frame with heavy wooden pegs

areas.description: Ruined stables left in neglect. The area once included a smithy, small cottages, hay storage, wagon storage, rooms for grooms, and similar structures, but these have collapsed into a maze of darkness and ruin. Soot and burned timbers from ancient fires are everywhere, and weeds grow through the rubbish. A giant weasel is sparring with three giant shrews over a pack of giant rats the shrews killed; if they smell fresh meat they attack it instead.
features: Ruined remains of former stables complex
Collapsed structures forming a maze of darkness and ruin
Soot and burned timbers from ancient fires

areas.description: Former quarters for gardeners and other staff, now ruined much like area 80. Berries, fruit trees, and vegetables grow wild here. During the growing season, characters can gather 1d6 weeks of rations, though most will spoil within a week or two unless preserved.
treasure: 1d6 weeks of rations during the growing season, though most spoil in a week or two unless preserved
features: Wild berries, fruit trees, and vegetables

areas.description: This area of rotten wooden buildings and weeds was once used for dog kennels. The wall here is patrolled by the humans' minotaur ally. If it notices intruders, it bellows to attract guards, throws rocks for 1d6 rounds or so, then charges down the nearest steps. In the northeast corner stands a rude lean-to of hides sheltering the half-eaten raw carcass of a deer, with animal parts strewn around.

encounters.count_note: patrols the wall; throws rocks before charging down the nearest steps

treasure: Necklace of 100 gp and 50 pp holed and strung together
features: Rotten wooden buildings from former dog kennels
Lean-to of hides in the northeast corner
Half-eaten deer carcass and scattered animal parts

areas.description: A 10-foot-deep moat surrounds the main house and is currently full. A simple wooden bridge once crossed it at the front entrance, but it burned down long ago, leaving only footing planks and timbers on each side. The moat is fed by a slow-moving stream entering under the north wall and exiting to the east. Each opening has an arched undercut with two sets of heavy iron bars, one on each side, to keep out intruders.
features: Burned-out remains of former wooden bridge
Slow-moving stream entering under the north wall and exiting east
Two sets of heavy iron bars at each opening

areas.description: The great iron double doors of the main house are firmly shut and barred from the inside and cannot be opened without something like a massive ram. High wide windows along the walls are barred with heavy iron, preventing entry by anything bigger than a squirrel. Forcing entry through the windows or by other noisy means is likely to attract attention from occupants. On the west side of the door, some blocks can be easily pulled from the wall where earlier invaders made a hole and the current occupants stacked the blocks back sloppily, allowing easy access into room 85. Nearby lie three wooden ramps, each about 24 feet long, made from collected timbers and iron hardware to let wagons cross the moat.
features: Great iron double doors barred from inside
High wide windows with heavy iron bars
Loose wall blocks on west side that can be removed to make an entry hole into 85
Three wooden wagon ramps about 24 feet long

areas.description: The main hall's lower floor is heavily used, with many hardwood timbers torn out or scuffed and dirt everywhere; the upstairs is cleaner and less damaged. A heavy iron chandelier hangs overhead for lanterns or torches, with smaller wall mounts throughout the manor. Skylights light the hall by day, and most rooms have outside windows. Two ornate stairs marked a and b rise about 20 feet to the second-floor balcony. Under the balcony are swinging double doors leading to the back. The main hall ceiling is about 30 feet high; other rooms are about 20 feet high. Recent bootprints in the dust lead everywhere. Some adjoining-room hardwood walls have been torn down in places. Horses can be heard in areas 86 and 87. As adventurers enter, three fighters in red surcoats over chain mail, carrying swords, are coming down the stairs, discussing whether raiding towns or waylaying travelers would make them richer. One is a woman with a longsword +1. They may try to lure intruders upstairs to area 106 while one fetches troops from room 88. A smith and two assistants from the stables join them if alerted by shouting or battle noise.

encounters.count_note: coming down the stairs; one has a longsword +1
smith from the stables joins if alerted
apprentice smiths from the stables join if alerted

features: Two ornate stairways (a and b) up to second-floor balcony
Swinging double doors under the balcony

areas.description: These rooms were once a library, music room, and gymnasium, but have been gutted and fitted with simple wooden stalls to serve as a smithy and stables. There are four riding horses, three mules, and three oxen here. Nearby are hay, feed bags, sacks of grain, and similar supplies. In one corner are a small traveling anvil, a pile of bricks, bags of coal, several hammers, other tools, and a few horseshoes. Two horses were lost to wandering monsters before the rest of the animals were moved inside for safety.

The south end of this area was once a posh sitting room, but the walls were knocked out to make room for wagons and equipment. Two wagons and two carts are stored here along with boxes, tarps, and other gear. The northern end was once a long dining hall. Old smashed tables and chairs are stacked neatly like firewood, and some thicker pieces are arranged like building timbers ready for use. There is ample room to move between the piles to reach the kitchen and prep room at the back.
features: Stacks of smashed tables and chairs
Thicker wood pieces arranged like building timbers
Open paths to the kitchen and prep room

areas.description: Formerly a prep room buffering the kitchen and dining room, this room now holds tables and chairs, usually strewn with dirty wooden plates, pewter or wooden mugs, and other items. A keg of cheap beer is propped on a table in one corner. A group of men are eating bread, meat, gravy, and cheap beer here. If fighting breaks out, two more men come from the kitchen. The leader wears chain mail and carries a sword; the others wear red-stained leather armor and carry various weapons including short swords, hand axes, and war hammers.
treasure: Each occupant has 2d6 gp on him

areas.description: A kitchen built to prepare large meals, with several ovens and stoves along the outer walls, plus plenty of tables and sinks. Dirty wooden dishes are common. Two men are here cooking stew on one of the stoves; if a fight breaks out they join the men in room 88. They are armed with hand axes and dressed in red-stained leather armor.

encounters.count_note: armed with hand axes; join room 88 if a fight breaks out

treasure: Each occupant has 2d6 gp on him

areas.description: A storeroom for dry foods. Bags of flour and other grains and vegetables are stacked here. A narrow stair leads down 10 feet to a cold room where fresh carcasses of venison, boar, rabbits, and other game hang on iron hooks.
features: Narrow stairs down 10 feet to cold room
Cold room with game carcasses on iron hooks

areas.description: Formerly a laundry room with buckets and tubs; now only long ropes for hanging laundry remain. Wooden shelves for linens and rugs stand empty. In one corner are two sealed buckets, one containing red paint and the other red varnish.
features: Empty wooden shelves for linens and rugs

areas.description: A wash room for people, clothes, and dishes. It contains bathtubs, cloth screens on wooden frames, and a table with bars of soap.
The rear exit of the manor. The door is a heavy security door with two iron bars that can be thrown across from the inside.
features: Two iron bars securing it from inside

areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, with inner partitions torn down. Two men here are resting after a fight with giant rats. They can still fight and will accost intruders as best they can. They are not wearing their leather armor, though they can put it on if given time.

encounters.count_note: resting after a fight with giant rats; not wearing leather armor unless given time

features: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops, most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The inner partitions have been torn down to enlarge the room.
features: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops, most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The inner partitions have been torn down to enlarge the room.
features: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops. As adventurers enter the hall around this room, a man comes out of the door and, unless stopped, ducks back inside and calls for help. Four men occupy this room, sleeping after watch. Their spears and hand axes are nearby. They are not currently wearing their red-stained leather armor, but will put it on if given time. Each carries coin.

encounters.count_note: sleeping after watch; not wearing armor unless given time

treasure: Each occupant has 2d6 gp on him
features: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down
